role: The right-hand-man (RHM) chat panel component that renders the conversational assistant region, including thread management, the chat log, proposed-action consent, locus indication, and voice/text input.
capabilities: Collapse/expand chat panel via minimize toggle
Display current model name in header (hidden in studio mode)
Start a new conversation and reopen past conversations from a thread selector
Record/stop a conversation session and launch a debrief walkthrough
Render the chat log with user/vi role labels and gold/working grade badges
Show and clear the currently indicated UI element locus
Text input with Enter-to-send and busy state
Voice input button supporting push-to-talk and auto-listen modes
substance_why: The file implements a complete, wired chat region with context hooks, local minimize state, thread controls, memory-loop buttons, a rendered message log, indication chip, proposal gate, and a dual-mode voice/text input.
interactions_present: Thread dropdown to reopen a conversation
Debrief button to review recorded sessions
Text input onChange and Enter key send
Mic button click for voice push-to-talk/auto-listen
interactions_missing: No disabled/empty-state guard on send when input is empty
No send failure, retry, or error feedback UI
No scroll-to-bottom or virtualization for long chat logs
No thread title editing or deletion
No loading/error state for thread list or debrief start
Voice mode selection was retired to A3 Settings and is no longer present here
performance_risks: Entire chat log re-renders on every keystroke because chatMsg lives in shared context and the component consumes broad context
chat.map uses array index as key, which can hurt reconciliation on edits or reordering
No virtualization or windowing for long chat histories
ProposeAffordance and the full panel re-render together on every context change
reuse_why: The chat organ, thread controls, memory loop, indication chip, and proposal gate are real and should be preserved, but the retired config gear, new studio prop, and broad context coupling mean the form should be rebuilt and split.
decomposition_hints: Split thread new/reopen controls into ConversationThreads
Split record/debrief memory loop into SessionRecorder or MemoryLoop
Split chat log rendering into ChatLog with its own scroll/virtualization
Keep ProposeAffordance as a separate child component
Isolate studio-mode conditional rendering at the composition level rather than inline
